behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is when the embedding model API produces a vector of dimension D, but the vector DB expects dimension D'. Mocking the embedding model would always return the expected dimension and hide this mismatch. We must call the real embedding API to discover what dimension it actually produces, then verify it matches the DB configuration.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test that calls the real embedding API with a test query string, retrieves the resulting embedding vector, and asserts its dimensionality matches the vector database index configuration. This catches real failures where the embedding model has been upgraded (different output dimension) or the API response format changed.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is the vector database returning results in the wrong order (e.g., insertion order, random, ascending instead of descending). Mocking the DB search would always return correctly ordered results and hide this ordering bug. We need a real or test vector database instance with known vectors and their similarity scores to verify the ordering constraint is actually enforced.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test that populates a local test vector database with N vectors of known positions, searches with a query vector, and asserts the returned results are strictly ordered by descending cosine similarity score. This catches real failures in DB configuration, search logic, or ranking pipeline.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is that the LLM does not understand the nuanced intent and produces the same or worse ranking than raw similarity scores. Mocking the LLM to return a different order would always pass, hiding the actual semantic understanding gap. Only calling the real LLM reveals whether it can parse nuance. The test outcome depends on the model's actual capabilities and current behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test that retrieves top-K results from the vector search for a nuanced query (e.g., 'recent user-friendly laptop under $800 with good battery life'), passes those results to the real LLM re-ranker with the original query, and asserts the LLM produces a measurably different ranking (different result at position 1 or different ordering) compared to raw similarity scores. Run multiple times to ensure the LLM consistently demonstrates semantic understanding, not random variation.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is the search returning low-similarity results instead of filtering them out. Mocking the vector DB to return empty results would always pass and hide failures in the similarity threshold logic. We need a real or test vector database populated with vectors sufficiently far from the test query, then verify the search applies its filtering threshold correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test that populates a local test vector database with vectors that are semantically far from a specific test query (e.g., database contains gardening products, query is 'laptop specifications'), executes the similarity search with an appropriate threshold, and asserts the result set is empty rather than returning low-similarity noise. Verify the threshold is respected.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Latency is only measurable with real network infrastructure: real HTTP round-trip time to the endpoint, real embedding API calls (non-zero latency), real vector database queries (potentially remote), and real LLM API calls (often 1-2s alone). Mocking all services would show zero latency and hide real SLA violations. The failure 'median latency exceeds 2s' only appears when using real external services with real network I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Create an end-to-end test that makes real HTTP POST requests to the REST API endpoint with various test queries (simple and nuanced), measures the wall-clock round-trip latency from request submission to receiving ranked results, runs multiple times (N≥10) to sample median latency, and asserts the median latency stays below 2 seconds. Include queries that exercise both the embedding, vector search, and LLM re-ranking paths.
